medications.drug_name: naloxone (take-home nasal spray or IM kit)
medications.indication_rationale: Immediate reversal of opioid-induced respiratory depression is the single most important bystander action if a kit is available, following the kit's instructions, while awaiting paramedics.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: If breathing, place in the recovery position; if not breathing normally, be ready to give rescue breaths; monitor continuously for re-sedation after naloxone, as its effect wears off faster than many opioids and repeat dosing or CPR may be needed.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Protects the airway and addresses the fact that naloxone's short duration means the patient can slip back into respiratory depression.

patient_education_points.point: This looks like a serious opioid overdose — his breathing is dangerously slow and he's not responding at all.
Confirm 000 is on the way, and if you have a naloxone kit, give it now following the instructions on the kit.
Put him in the recovery position on his side if he's breathing, and be ready to give rescue breaths if he stops.
Even if he wakes up after the naloxone, keep watching him closely — the naloxone can wear off before the drugs do, and he could become unresponsive again, needing another dose.
Gather any pill packets or drugs nearby to give to the paramedics so they know what he may have taken.

follow_up_plan.interval: Immediate handover to paramedics for ongoing monitoring, transport, and hospital observation for 12-24 hours
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Respiratory status, need for repeat naloxone, toxicology screen results, and mandatory psychiatric/addiction medicine review
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Not applicable — already an emergency requiring immediate action

must_include_items: Immediate naloxone administration instruction if a kit is available
Explicit warning about re-sedation risk after naloxone wears off
acceptable_alternatives: If no naloxone kit is available, focus on airway positioning, rescue breathing, and awaiting paramedics
errors_of_omission: Failing to instruct naloxone use if available
No warning about naloxone wearing off
errors_of_commission: Suggesting the bystander wait to see if he wakes up on his own before acting
Recommending anything other than immediate emergency action as the first response
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Any delay in instructing naloxone administration/airway support to gather more history is a critical, life-threatening safety failure in this time-critical scenario.
